days.title: Secret Entrance
days.essence: Track a dial on a 0–99 circle through signed rotations, counting landings on the home position. Part 2 also counts every pass over home, which the solution does with floor-division arithmetic rather than stepping one tick at a time.

days.title: Gift Shop
days.essence: Sum IDs in given numeric ranges whose decimal digits are a shorter block repeated. Part 1 only flags blocks repeated exactly twice; part 2 generalizes to any divisor-length periodic repetition.

days.title: Lobby
days.essence: Form the largest number from a battery bank by switching on a fixed count of digits without reordering. The solution greedily picks the maximum digit in each remaining window; part 2 raises the count from 2 to 12 digits, demanding the windowed left-to-right greedy be exactly right.

days.title: Printing Department
days.essence: On a 2D grid, a paper roll is reachable when at most three of its eight neighbors are also rolls. Part 1 counts reachable rolls once; part 2 repeatedly removes all reachable rolls in rounds until none remain, totalling the removals.

days.title: Cafeteria
days.essence: Merge a set of overlapping inclusive ID ranges by sorting and coalescing. Part 1 counts which listed ingredients fall in any range; part 2 sums the total size of the merged fresh ranges.

days.title: Trash Compactor
days.essence: Evaluate add/multiply problems laid out as vertical columns of stacked numbers with the operator on the bottom row. The difficulty is purely in reconstructing the columns; part 2 reinterprets the layout by reading digits vertically down each column, making the parsing the whole puzzle.

days.title: Laboratories
days.essence: A descending tachyon beam forks left and right at every splitter, recursing down the grid. Part 1 counts distinct splitter positions reached, but part 2's count of resulting timelines grows exponentially, so memoizing each splitter position is what keeps it tractable.

days.title: Playground
days.essence: Repeatedly connect the closest pair of 3D junction boxes with union-find, Kruskal-style. Part 1 multiplies the three largest circuit sizes after 1000 connections; part 2 keeps connecting until everything merges into one circuit and reports the final joining pair.

days.title: Movie Theater
days.essence: Pick two red tiles as opposite corners and maximize the rectangle area over all pairs. Part 2 restricts to rectangles lying entirely inside a rectilinear polygon, requiring point-in-polygon ray-crossing tests plus segment-intersection checks against the boundary.

days.title: Factory
days.essence: Buttons XOR fixed light masks; part 1 finds the fewest presses to hit a target light pattern via BFS over bitmask states. Part 2 adds per-light joltage counts, solved by a recursive parity bifurcation (subtracting a GF(2) solution and halving the targets) with memoization, which is the hard insight of the year.

days.title: Reactor
days.essence: Count every distinct path through a directed acyclic device network from a source to a sink, memoizing path counts per node so the exponential number of routes is summed in linear time. Part 2 chains the same counter through fixed waypoints and multiplies the segment counts.

days.title: Christmas Tree Farm
days.essence: Decide which regions can hold all their required presents, a 2D packing problem with rotations and flips. Rather than truly pack, the solution exploits the specific input: a region is feasible exactly when an area lower bound and a loose 3x3-cell upper bound agree, so the two cheap bounds coincide and pin down the answer.
